title: deploy: surface actual backend error body on 401/4xx instead of canned message
description: The hosted deploy command catches 401 and prints a hardcoded message without reading the response body. This hides the actual backend error and makes debugging impossible. Fix: always read and print the response body on any non-2xx response, including 401.

features: In src/commands/deploy.js, in the deployHosted() function: remove the early-return 401 special case that prints the canned message WITHOUT reading the body.
Replace with a unified error handler: for ANY non-2xx response, attempt to parse the JSON body, then print: 'Deploy failed (HTTP <status>): <data.error || data.message || JSON.stringify(data)>'
For 401 specifically: AFTER printing the actual backend message, also print the hint on a new line: 'Hint: run links register --force to get a new API key.'
This way the actual backend error is always visible, plus the hint is still shown for 401.
Also add a --verbose flag to the deploy command: when set, print the full raw response body regardless of status (useful for debugging unexpected responses).

notes: The 401 case currently returns before response.json() is called — that is the bug. Read the body first, THEN branch on status. The --verbose flag should print raw response body (JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)) before the error message when status is non-2xx.
